summary: FuelPro backend regression — 32 pytest cases over health, auth (register/login/me, dup 409, 401), plans+subscription, Stripe checkout creation, Stripe status (xfail), M-PESA mocked STK push, callback handler, cloud sync (per-user scoping), user-data, EPRA fuel-prices, audit log, public receipt verify, catch-all stub, and auth-required endpoints. 31 passed, 1 xfail = real backend issue: Stripe /payments/stripe/status/{session_id} fails after a successful create.

critical.issue: POST /api/payments/stripe/checkout returns a real cs_test_* session_id and a valid https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/... URL, but the very next GET /payments/stripe/status/{session_id} returns 502 with Stripe error 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'. Reproduced consistently with 3s sleep between create and status. This breaks the end-to-end paywall: the StripeReturnHandler in the frontend polls /payments/stripe/status to activate the subscription, so users will never get upgraded even after paying. RCA hypothesis: emergentintegrations.StripeCheckout instantiated per request may bind to a different test account when querying than when creating (key/account mismatch between create_checkout_session and get_checkout_status). Verify the lib internally pins the same Stripe account/key for both calls — possibly by reusing one StripeCheckout instance, persisting the api_key returned by the lib, or passing session metadata back to status.

minor.issue: Invalid phone like '12345' returns 422 (Pydantic min_length=9) instead of the custom 400 with a 'valid Kenyan number' message. UX nit — frontend gets generic 422 validation envelope instead of human-friendly error.
Returns 502 (Stripe upstream error wrapped) rather than 404. Acceptable but could be normalised.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix Stripe status flow. The emergentintegrations StripeCheckout instance for status() does not recognise sessions created by another instance. Either (a) cache one StripeCheckout instance at module level and reuse it for both create+status, (b) use Stripe's REST API directly with the same sk_test_emergent for retrieval, or (c) escalate to emergentintegrations maintainers. Without this fix, /payments/stripe/checkout looks healthy but the subscription will NEVER activate end-to-end.
Optional polish: catch Pydantic ValidationError in /mpesa/stk-push and re-raise as 400 with a friendlier message; convert 502 from Stripe 'No such checkout.session' into 404.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 974 lines and mixes auth, payments, M-PESA, sync, EPRA, audit, and stubs. Should be split into modules (auth.py, payments_stripe.py, mpesa.py, sync.py, prices.py) for maintainability.

Stripe checkout uses `host_url = str(request.base_url)` for webhook_url. Behind ingress this resolves to the internal host, not the public URL. Webhooks from Stripe may not reach the backend if Stripe is given the internal URL. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL or a dedicated env var.

POST /api/sync/{collection} blindly replaces the entire user collection with provided items (delete_many+insert_many). No transaction → if insert_many partially fails the user loses all data. Wrap in a Mongo session/transaction or use bulk upserts keyed on item.id.

GET /api/user-data fallback to `request.query_params.get('user_id')` is a soft user-spoofing vector — any anon caller can set ?user_id=<known-id> and read another user's user_data. The x-user-id header has the same problem. Either remove the anon fallback for sensitive endpoints, or namespace anon writes under an opaque token.

/api/verify/receipt/{receipt} uppercases the input only when matching mpesa_receipt — M-PESA receipts are case-sensitive uppercase in production, but stored receipts come from the callback as-is. Verify case consistency.
Catch-all stub returns ok:true for any path including obvious typos, masking real client bugs in production. Consider logging+returning 404 in non-development environments.
JWT_EXPIRE_HOURS=720 (30 days) is long; consider refresh tokens.
Daraja `_timestamp` uses UTC+3 manually — should use Africa/Nairobi tz to be DST-safe (Kenya doesn't observe DST so OK, but flag for completeness).
register/login responses include user dict but UserOut uses ConfigDict(extra='ignore'); password_hash is correctly excluded because UserOut is built explicitly — good.
Indexes are created in startup; users.email unique is correct, but a TEST_*@fuelpro.app account is being created on every test run with no cleanup → users collection will grow.

success_rate.backend: 97% (31/32 passed, 1 xfail = real Stripe status bug)

test_credentials: Fresh users created per run: TEST_<uuid8>@fuelpro.app / password123. No seeded credentials needed.
seed_data_creation: Per-test users TEST_*@fuelpro.app, two M-PESA mock transactions, two cloud-sync items for user_a, one for user_b, one audit-log entry per user_a. Not cleaned up — recommend a periodic Mongo cleanup of TEST_* emails or a teardown fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_fuelpro_backend.py is the regression suite — run with `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_fuelpro_backend.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. The Stripe status xfail test will become a PASS once the emergentintegrations create→retrieve account mismatch is fixed. M-PESA STK push intentionally mocked (Daraja creds blank) — `mocked=true` in response is correct per spec. /api/mpesa/stk-callback was tested with an unknown CheckoutRequestID to confirm it accepts the Safaricom payload shape without 500ing; end-to-end upgrade chain not tested because mock STK push doesn't expose CheckoutRequestID to the caller.
rca of the issue: Stripe status 'No such checkout.session': create_checkout_session in emergentintegrations StripeCheckout likely uses a server-side rotating/test account key while get_checkout_status uses a different key (each StripeCheckout instance independently). Confirmed by hitting Stripe directly and getting req_* error referencing the exact cs_test_* id that was just minted 3s earlier. Reproduction: register user → POST /payments/stripe/checkout {plan:'pro',...} → record session_id+url → wait → GET /payments/stripe/status/{session_id} → 502 'No such checkout.session'. Mitigation options listed in action_items.
